author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine)
content:
EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT / ADMISSION H&P — UPMC
Date/Time: 5/2/2027, 3:30 PM
Patient: Brooks, Janet M (MRN-90216077), 55F

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Abdominal distension and shortness of breath; 3 months of unintentional weight loss.

HPI: 55-year-old woman with alcohol-related cirrhosis (ongoing alcohol use) presents with progressive abdominal distension and dyspnea. She and her sister report ~20 lb of UNINTENTIONAL weight loss over the past 3 months with decreased appetite — she had been undergoing an outpatient workup with hepatology (dedicated liver imaging was pending). She denies fever or chills at home. Over the past week, increasing abdominal girth and exertional breathlessness.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Alcohol-related cirrhosis (portal hypertension, prior ascites, encephalopathy on lactulose/rifaximin). Depression (untreated). 30-pack-year smoking.

SOCIAL: Active alcohol use. Lives alone; estranged from her two adult children (recently beginning to reconcile). Sister Carol is closely involved.

EXAM (initial): T 36.7 C, BP 100/60, HR 95, RR 18, SpO2 97% RA. Cachectic-appearing. Abdomen distended with fluid wave, non-tender. Decreased breath sounds at both bases. Alert, oriented; no asterixis at triage.

DATA:
- Labs (LAB001): WBC 12.0, Hgb 8.6, Plt 80; Na 132, Cr 1.4; T-bili 4.2, AST 50, ALT 60, INR 1.9; albumin 2.5; lactate 2.2. MELD-Na ~25.
- Diagnostic paracentesis (PROC001): 86 PMN/uL, no organisms on Gram stain — NEGATIVE for SBP; SAAG >1.1.
- Abdominal US (IMG001): cirrhosis, splenomegaly, new ascites, portal hypertension; no focal liver mass on this study.
- CXR (IMG002): bilateral pleural effusions (hepatic hydrothorax).

ED COURSE: Shortly after arrival the patient developed MASSIVE HEMATEMESIS with hemodynamic instability. Massive transfusion protocol activated; GI and ICU notified; preparing for intubation and emergent endoscopy. (See ICU admission note.)

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
55F with decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis and 3 months of involuntary weight loss, now with acute variceal hemorrhage.
- Resuscitate (MTP); intubate for airway protection; emergent EGD.
- Octreotide + IV PPI; ceftriaxone for variceal-bleed/SBP prophylaxis.
- Admit to MICU.
- The involuntary-weight-loss workup (initiated outpatient) to be continued once stabilized.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Olivia Reyes, MD (Emergency Medicine). 5/2/2027.
